title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You hear two people talking about a school play.
extracts.audio_script: Girl: Are you coming to the school play tomorrow? || Boy: I want to, but I haven't bought a ticket yet. || Girl: There are still some left, but not many. || Boy: [8]I'll buy one at lunchtime today because they may sell out before the evening[/8]. My little sister wants to come too, so I should get two. It also means we will not have to spend extra money unnecessarily. I suppose it is a small change, but it makes the situation much easier.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Are you coming to the school play tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I want to, but I haven't bought a ticket yet.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: There are still some left, but not many.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I'll buy one at lunchtime today because they may sell out before the evening. My little sister wants to come too, so I should get two. It also means we will not have to spend extra money unnecessarily. I suppose it is a small change, but it makes the situation much easier.

questions.question: What will the boy do at lunchtime?
questions.explanation: The boy says he will buy tickets at lunchtime because they may sell out.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a cycle ride.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: How was your cycle ride yesterday? || Man: Nice, but harder than I expected. I chose the hill route to avoid the busy road. || Woman: Was the weather bad? || Man: No, [9]the problem was that my bike seat was too low, so my legs hurt by the end[/9]. I fixed it when I got home. That should make the plan easier and help everyone know what to expect. I think that is the most sensible choice after looking at the other possibilities.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: How was your cycle ride yesterday?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Nice, but harder than I expected. I chose the hill route to avoid the busy road.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Was the weather bad?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: No, the problem was that my bike seat was too low, so my legs hurt by the end. I fixed it when I got home. That should make the plan easier and help everyone know what to expect. I think that is the most sensible choice after looking at the other possibilities.

questions.question: What made the cycle ride difficult?

options.B: the position of the bike seat

questions.explanation: The man says his bike seat was too low, which made his legs hurt.

extracts.context: You hear a boy talking to his mother about dinner.
extracts.audio_script: Mother: What shall we have for dinner tonight? I can make pasta if you like. || Boy: We had pasta yesterday. What about fish? || Mother: The fish shop closes early on Mondays. || Boy: Then [10]let's use the vegetables in the fridge and make soup[/10]. It will be quick, and we won't need to go shopping. It means there should be fewer problems when the time comes to do it. I would rather decide now than leave everything until the last minute.

dialogue.speaker: mother
dialogue.text: What shall we have for dinner tonight? I can make pasta if you like.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: We had pasta yesterday. What about fish?

dialogue.speaker: mother
dialogue.text: The fish shop closes early on Mondays.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Then let's use the vegetables in the fridge and make soup. It will be quick, and we won't need to go shopping. It means there should be fewer problems when the time comes to do it. I would rather decide now than leave everything until the last minute.

questions.question: What do they decide to make for dinner?
questions.explanation: The boy suggests using the vegetables in the fridge to make soup.

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a town festival.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you helping at the town festival this year? || Woman: Yes, but not on the cake stall this time. I did that last year. || Man: Are you selling tickets? || Woman: No, [11]I'm helping children paint masks in the art tent[/11]. It sounds more fun, and I won't have to stand in the sun all afternoon. That way, we can enjoy it more and avoid rushing around later. It is not perfect, but it seems better than the other choices we had.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you helping at the town festival this year?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but not on the cake stall this time. I did that last year.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you selling tickets?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: No, I'm helping children paint masks in the art tent. It sounds more fun, and I won't have to stand in the sun all afternoon. That way, we can enjoy it more and avoid rushing around later. It is not perfect, but it seems better than the other choices we had.

questions.question: What will the woman do at the festival?
questions.explanation: The woman says she is helping children paint masks in the art tent.

extracts.context: You hear a man leaving a message for a friend.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Hi Jack, it's Sam. About tomorrow's tennis match, I'm afraid I can't meet you at ten. || Man: My dentist appointment has moved to the morning, and it may take a while. || Man: [12]Can we play at half past twelve instead?[/12] The courts are free then, and I can bring the balls as usual. The important thing is to keep it simple and not make extra work. It should be fine as long as everybody remembers the new arrangement.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Hi Jack, it's Sam. About tomorrow's tennis match, I'm afraid I can't meet you at ten.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: My dentist appointment has moved to the morning, and it may take a while.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Can we play at half past twelve instead? The courts are free then, and I can bring the balls as usual. The important thing is to keep it simple and not make extra work. It should be fine as long as everybody remembers the new arrangement.

questions.question: What change does Sam want to make?

options.A: He wants to play at a different place.
options.C: He wants Jack to bring the balls.

questions.explanation: Sam asks if they can play at half past twelve instead of ten, so he wants to play later.

extracts.context: You hear two friends talking about a holiday photo.
extracts.audio_script: Girl: That's a great photo of you by the lake. Did your brother take it? || Boy: No, a woman who was walking past offered to help. || Girl: The light looks perfect. || Boy: Yes. [13]I like the photo because you can see the mountains clearly behind me[/13]. In most of my pictures they were hidden by clouds. I am glad we checked the details before making the final decision. That gives us enough time to prepare without changing everything else.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: That's a great photo of you by the lake. Did your brother take it?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: No, a woman who was walking past offered to help.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: The light looks perfect.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Yes. I like the photo because you can see the mountains clearly behind me. In most of my pictures they were hidden by clouds. I am glad we checked the details before making the final decision. That gives us enough time to prepare without changing everything else.

questions.question: Why does the boy like the photo?
questions.explanation: The boy says he likes the photo because the mountains can be seen clearly behind him.
